title: 001. Sahras Kür
author: Unknown
summary: Sara, an ice skater competing in Tokyo, performs a revolutionary and provocative free programme after a poor showing in her compulsory elements. Choosing a 'sexy, flexy' routine set to Serge Gainsbourg, she executes unprecedented moves, including extreme frontbends and a record-breaking 360-degree oversplit. The narrative focuses heavily on the technical aspects of her flexibility and the shock of the audience as she sheds her skirt to reveal a scandalous lack of regulation attire. This story fits the corpus by emphasizing the boundary-pushing nature of human flexibility and the resulting social friction when such 'unlimited' movement is presented in a conservative, regulated environment. The focus on anatomical feats over traditional sportsmanship is a key thematic marker.

title: 002. A Dream
author: Unknown
summary: The narrator attends an exclusive party where a 'latex doll' is the central attraction. Two bodybuilders manipulate a woman encased in metallic blue latex, moving her through impossible oversplits, 180-degree head rotations, and extreme torso torsions. The performance concludes with her being folded into a tiny, transparent box with windows, where she is left as a decorative object. This story is a strong fit for the corpus due to its focus on the objectification of hyper-flexible bodies and the surreal, almost mechanical nature of the stretching. It explores the aesthetic of 'human furniture' and the technical achievement of fitting a human into a container that defies their skeletal logic, framed through a lens of silent, mesmerizing performance.

title: 003. Kautschuk Girl Contest
author: Unknown
summary: A sex film expert travels to Los Angeles to judge a casting for 'rubber girls' intended for erotic productions. Assisted by his own flexible production assistant, Kathy, the narrator observes a series of elite acrobatic and contortionist performances. Highlighting the 'world sensation' of a woman undressing inside a 50cm glass cube, the story blends professional gymnastic standards with explicit erotic voyeurism. It fits the corpus by presenting flexibility as a professional commodity and a source of extreme aesthetic fascination. The detailed descriptions of 'pink shots' and specific anatomical measurements emphasize the intersection of physical discipline and erotic fetishization, a core pillar of this narrative style.

title: 004. Anja
author: Unknown
summary: Set in 1968 Paris, the narrator discovers his romantic interest, Anja, is a highly trained contortionist. Initially presented as a simple gymnast, her extreme abilities are revealed during a secret training session and a later surprise New Year's Eve performance where she executes reverse lotus positions and deep backbends. The narrative follows a classic discovery arc, where the narrator’s growing affection is fueled by his fascination with Anja's hidden physical gifts. This fits the corpus through its focus on 'discovery' and the personal, intimate nature of the flexibility demonstration. It grounds extreme contortion in a romantic context, highlighting the lifelong bond formed through the shared appreciation of physical limits and 'erotic stretching.'

title: 005. Adagio Pair
author: Unknown
summary: Sandra and Alexei, a circus adagio pair, maintain their competitive edge through grueling nightly training sessions. The story focuses on Alexei's use of a custom-designed stretching machine that uses weights and pulleys to force Sandra into extreme oversplits over long durations. The narrative emphasizes the physical toll and 'sacrifices' required to achieve such performance standards, framing the intensity as a byproduct of their professional and romantic relationship. It fits the corpus by focusing on the technical and mechanical aspects of achieving extreme flexibility. The presence of specialized equipment and the clinical focus on 'degrees of improvement' align with the corpus's obsession with the procedural side of anatomical deformation.

title: 006. Underground
author: Unknown
summary: A narrator stalks a beautiful woman from the underground to her contortion agency, where he eventually meets Heidi, the owner and an 'anatomical miracle.' Heidi reveals she has been aware of his observation and initiates a game where she is delivered to his home inside a small transparent sphere, encased in silver latex. The story details her ability to perform 360-degree torsions and extreme frontbends in his living room. This is a prototypical corpus story, focusing on the discovery of hidden flexibility and the 'packing' of the female form into restrictive, aesthetically pleasing containers. The blend of latex fetishism and hyper-mobility reinforces the core themes of the narrator's voyeuristic fascination with human limits.

title: 007. Vodoo Magic
author: Unknown
summary: The narrator uses a red rubber 'doll' to enact a 'voodoo' revenge against his bullying boss, Daniela, twisting and knotting the doll into extreme, unnatural shapes. The narrative follows various indignities, including stuffing the doll into lockers and washing machines. A final twist reveals that the doll is his wife, Daniela, and the entire scenario is a weekend fetish roleplay involving skin-tight rubber suits and extreme contortion. This story fits the corpus by exploring the domestic life of the 'flexible woman' and the objectification inherent in fetish roleplay. It highlights the aesthetic pleasure of 'deforming' the human silhouette and the specific dynamics of power and submission mediated through extreme physical flexibility.

title: 008. Susi meets an old friend
author: Unknown
summary: Susi reunites with her old friend Peter and invites him over, knowing his lifelong fascination with gymnastics. She performs a series of private flexibility demonstrations, wearing a mesh leotard and high heels, which culminates in a request for Peter to help her fit into a small plexiglass box. The story focuses on the trust and physical collaboration required to achieve extreme 'packed' positions. It fits the corpus style by depicting a normal social interaction that pivots into an intensive study of flexibility. The focus on Peter's role as the 'facilitator' who locks Susi into her contortion box highlights the typical corpus dynamic of the male observer as a technical assistant to the female's anatomical performance.

title: 009. Doreen
author: Unknown
summary: Doreen, a tiny 'natural' contortionist, participates in an elaborate public performance called the 'Unbreakable Doll.' Treated as a literal mannequin, she is manipulated by the audience and the narrator into triple folds and extreme backbends before being packed into a series of transparent boxes, including a 'table' box containing two women twisted together. The narrative is exhaustive in its technical detail, covering costume design (zentai), anatomical limits (360-degree rotation), and the logistical challenges of human packing. This is a foundational corpus entry, emphasizing the 'human furniture' trope and the clinical, voyeuristic perfection of the hyper-flexible form. It showcases the ultimate transition of the flexible woman from a human agent to a spectacular, malleable object.

title: 010. Unglaublicher Abend
author: Unknown
summary: While visiting relatives in San Francisco, the narrator falls for Carol, a pretty blonde with exceptionally long legs. Their romantic evening takes a turn when Carol reveals she was a professional circus acrobat and performs a private warm-up routine for the narrator in a shiny silver bodysuit. The story focuses on the narrator's excitement as he assists her with extreme oversplits and backbends. It fits the corpus discovery arc perfectly, where a conventional romantic interest is 'revealed' to possess extraordinary flexibility. The emphasis on the aesthetic qualities of her bodysuit and the tactile nature of the assisted stretching aligns with the corpus's core interest in the eroticization of gymnastic and circus-level mobility.

title: 011. Feierabend
author: Unknown
summary: The narrator observes a group of four young girls performing gymnastics in a residential yard after work. The scene transitions from casual play to a structured demonstration of extreme flexibility, featuring jumping splits and assisted oversplits. The narrative meticulously details the physical mechanics of the positions, specifically the 'men's split' and the technical way the girls manipulate each other's limbs to achieve greater depth. This story fits the corpus through its focus on the 'hidden' nature of extreme flexibility discovered in a mundane, everyday setting. It emphasizes the technical execution of anatomical feats, though the adolescent age of the subjects and the lack of a formal performance setting place it in the 'partial' band. The focus remains on the aesthetic of the 'perfect split' and the observer's fascination with physical limits.

title: 012. The examiner
author: Unknown
summary: A stressful day for an office manager concludes with a birthday surprise arranged by his colleagues. The 'examiner' he is sent to assist, Barbara, reveals herself to be a professional variety artist. The narrative centers on a discovery arc where a professional facade is dropped to reveal extreme contortionist abilities, beginning with a foot-behind-head demonstration in an office chair and escalating to a candlelit performance in a bikini. Key performance elements include deep backbends and the ability to touch feet under the chin. This fits the corpus perfectly as a 'secret talent' narrative, where the eroticized nature of the flexibility is framed as a reward for the narrator's diligence. The focus on the contrast between the severe office environment and the fluid, hyper-mobile body is a central thematic fit.

title: 013. Zlata and Yogini
author: Unknown
summary: The famous contortionist Zlata observes a private demonstration by an elite Indian yogini, Miss Deva, in a ritualistic cave setting. The narrative focuses on the brutal physical reality of extreme flexibility training, emphasizing the role of assistants who use maximum force to push the body into impossible configurations. Key scenes include a rotating lotus position and a triple fold where the yogini's head is pulled beyond her knees through the application of the assistants' full body weight. This entry fits the corpus by focusing on the 'procedural' and 'technical' aspects of contortion, treating the human body as a malleable material to be coiled and stretched. The clinical observation of pain and the absolute dedication to 'unlimited mobility' align with the corpus’s core interest in the limits of human anatomy.

title: 014. A Girl from Omsk
author: Unknown
summary: Olga, an untrained schoolteacher, discovers her own dormant hyper-flexibility during a conversation with her friend Tanya, a rhythmic gymnastics coach. The narrative explores the physical impact of 'breaking' one's limits, as Olga forces herself into a perfect straddle split despite the initial pain. A subsequent sequence involving Tanya being pulled into an extreme triple fold emphasizes the 'rubber-like' potential of the human frame. The story fits the corpus style by highlighting the 'natural' contortionist trope—a woman who possesses world-class mobility without formal training. The focus on the 'dizziness of pain' and the subsequent craving for further stretching explores the addictive, obsessive nature of flexibility. It grounds the anatomical feats in a narrative of personal crisis and the discovery of a hidden physical identity.

title: 015. Summer 2007
author: Unknown
summary: A physics student falls for Julia, a Russian exchange student and rhythmic gymnast. The narrative follows a classic discovery arc where the narrator's interest in Julia is deepened by her athletic prowess and flexibility. The story culminates in private sessions where the narrator assists Julia with her training, involving deep backbends and forced splits. This fits the corpus through its focus on the 'outsider' (narrator) being introduced to the world of extreme flexibility by a practitioner. The detailed descriptions of the 'pulling and squeezing' involved in assisted stretching, framed within a developing romantic relationship, emphasize the corpus's focus on the tactile and aesthetic pleasure derived from physical manipulation. The story successfully blends mundane student life with the intensive, obsessive study of human mobility.

title: 016. Zlata in Danger
author: Unknown
summary: Valerie (Zlata) uses her extreme flexibility to infiltrate a high-security building through narrow ventilation shafts. The narrative focuses on the technical application of contortion, describing 180-degree backbends required to navigate hairpin turns in air pipes. A central scene involves Valerie performing a private 'Olympic' routine on a mahogany conference table, including a 270-degree oversplit and a head-through-legs fold, before packing herself into a small suitcase to escape. This story fits the corpus by presenting flexibility as a 'superpower' or professional tool. The focus on the 'second skin' catsuit and the logistics of human packing (folding into the embryo position for hours) reinforces the corpus’s fascination with the objectification and containment of the flexible female form within a high-stakes, professional espionage context.

title: 017. My rhythmic gymnastics sisters
author: Unknown
summary: A 14-year-old boy, Sandy, is subjected to an intensive 'flexibility session' by his older sister and her friend, both rhythmic gymnasts. The narrative details a ritualistic sequence involving sensory deprivation and the use of a white lycra catsuit. The girls perform passive stretching on Sandy, forcing him into straddle splits and yoga lotus positions, culminating in an 'underwater contortion' experiment. This fits the corpus by exploring the dynamics of physical control and the 'shaping' of a body by more experienced practitioners. The focus on the 'sensation' of being stretched and the aesthetic of the 'white cocoon' highlights the corpus’s interest in the intersection of gymnastic discipline and light fetishistic elements. It frames the development of flexibility as a communal, domestic, and intensely sensory experience.

title: 018. A Girl named Bio
author: Unknown
summary: BIO, a 'natural' contortionist discovered in the Amazon and raised in the cabaret circuit, is the subject of intense anatomical fascination. The narrative focuses on her extraordinary physical anomalies, such as a 360-degree spinal torsion and a waist that can be compressed to 13cm. Key details include her being transported as an infant in a violin case, establishing her from birth as a malleable object. The performance scenes emphasize the technical 'impossibility' of her movements, with observers touching her 'coiled innards' to verify the reality of the deformation. This is a quintessential corpus entry, focusing on the 'freak of nature' trope and the clinical fascination with extreme anatomical limits. The transition from human agent to a 'work of art' that can be packed and measured is a core thematic marker.

title: 019. Oona
author: Unknown
summary: A single man discovers that his cleaning lady, Oona, is a highly skilled 'snake girl' from Africa. The narrative follows an extensive discovery arc where Oona demonstrates a series of traditional and modern contortion poses, including the 'Sun Wheel' and extreme backbends where her hips sink to the floor in front of her head. The story details the narrator’s increasing obsession as he assists her with stretching and purchases professional gear for her demonstrations. It fits the corpus perfectly through its focus on the 'private demo' and the 'discovery' of world-class flexibility in a domestic setting. The analytical focus on her 'infinitely long legs' and the technical improvement of her neck mobility through assisted pressure aligns with the corpus’s core interests in anatomical malleability and the observer's role as a facilitator.

title: 020. Train
author: Unknown
summary: The narrator visits a gymnastics training hall, expecting to be repulsed by the 'absurd' stretching but instead becoming transfixed. The story focuses on a girl in a green unitard performing extreme routines under the guidance of her trainer. Key highlights include a triple fold where her head emerges from between her thighs and a backbend where her head touches her buttocks. The narrative emphasizes the 'ambition' and 'discipline' of the contortionists, who push their trainers for more intense stretches. This story is a strong fit for the corpus as it documents the 'conversion' of a skeptic into a fascinated observer through the mesmerizing sight of physical limits being surpassed. The clinical focus on the 'marshmallow' nature of the spine and the performative discipline of the athlete are central corpus themes.

title: 021. Magic Contortion
author: Unknown
summary: Jessica purchases a 'magic contortion kit' that merges her with a black full-body suit, rendering her essentially boneless. She then signs up for a stage show where two female assistants push her body into impossible limits, including 540-degree splits, a 360-degree human knot, and an extreme triple fold. The narrative culminates in her being packed into a cube exactly matched to her body's water displacement volume, emphasizing the complete removal of empty space. This entry fits the corpus strongly by pushing the anatomical limit to a surreal, 'rubber girl' extreme. It highlights the aesthetic of the hyper-flexible female form manipulated entirely by outside agents for public viewing, while the clinical measurement of her body volume reinforces the objectification trope.

title: 022. The Fly
author: Unknown
summary: While traveling on business in Cairo, the narrator becomes infatuated with a woman he spots on the airplane. Later, he attends a hotel stage show and discovers she is an elite contortionist, watching her perform extreme backbends and a triple fold on a rotating platform. When they accidentally meet at the bar afterward, he boldly confesses his lifelong contortion fetish and describes an erotic stretching fantasy involving her. The story is a quintessential 'discovery' arc combined with intense voyeuristic observation. It fits the corpus style perfectly by framing extreme flexibility as an eroticized spectacle and detailing the narrator's transition from a passive observer to an active, desiring participant.

title: 023. Contortion Duo Erotica
author: Unknown
summary: A photographer visits an exclusive London club to watch Pia and Mae, Thai twins who perform highly eroticized contortion acts. The narrative extensively details their stage routine, which includes deep oversplits on mirrored glass blocks and extreme backbends while wearing high heels and minimal costuming. The narrator forms an immediate romantic and sexual bond with Pia, leading to a private table dance and eventually a lasting relationship built around 'splits love.' This story strongly aligns with the corpus through its intense focus on the professional exhibition of flexibility and the direct linking of acrobatic talent with sexual prowess. The precise measurements of props and angles highlight the clinical fetishism characteristic of the genre.

title: 024. Woman without borders
author: Unknown
summary: After meeting a contortionist named Carina in an online chatroom, the narrator receives a surprise package containing Carina herself, tightly folded inside a glass box. Once unpacked, she performs a private demonstration, casually exhibiting boundless flexibility, including 360-degree waist twists and joint dislocations. The story peaks at a party where she is revealed from beneath the narrator's fur coat to shock his guests. This entry perfectly captures the 'human package' and 'discovery' themes of the corpus. The playful, casual treatment of extreme, impossible anatomy—such as the 360-degree twist—combined with the objectification of being delivered as a birthday present makes it a strong thematic fit.

title: 025. Anna
author: Unknown
summary: The narrator runs into a childhood friend, Anna, who invites him to her apartment for a private demonstration of her advanced contortion skills. The narrative is highly focused on tactile observation, with the narrator feeling her vertebrae and manipulating her spine as she folds into extreme backbends and triple folds on a dining table. The story reveals that her flexibility is a hereditary trait passed down from her circus-performer mother. This is a foundational corpus entry due to its intense clinical focus on anatomy. The tactile exploration of the 'rubber-like' spine and the romantic undercurrents developed through the physical manipulation of the female form strongly represent the genre's core aesthetics.
